mnemonics.title: SPIRAL Phases
mnemonics.content: P lan, R isk analysis, E ngineering, E valuation - each cycle PR.EEDS progress

mnemonics.title: V-Model Mapping
mnemonics.content: V ertical sides show Re quire ments → Co ding (left), Tes ting (right) → Deploy ment (bottom)

mnemonics.title: Agile Values
mnemonics.content: I ndividuals over processes, Wo rking software over documentation, C ustomer collaboration over contracts, R esponding to change over following a plan

acronyms.usage: Used for projects requiring quick delivery with high user involvement
Collaborative approach bringing developers and users together in workshops
Practice combining development and operations for continuous delivery
Extension of Waterfall emphasizing testing at each phase
Agile framework using sprints, daily standups, and product backlogs

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a spiral starting from center (concept) expanding outward, with each loop representing a development cycle getting larger and more complete
Draw a V shape - left descending side shows development phases, right ascending side shows corresponding testing phases, bottom connects them
Think of building blocks stacked progressively - each new block adds functionality on top of existing working modules
Picture a circular track where each lap (sprint) produces a complete mini-version of the product, getting better each time

analogies.analogy: Like writing a rough draft, then revising it multiple times, each time making it more polished and complete while identifying potential issues early
Like an architect building a small-scale model of a building before constructing the actual structure to get client feedback
Like a rapid prototyping workshop where designers quickly create mockups, get instant feedback, and iterate to final design
Like an iterative cooking process where you taste and adjust the recipe throughout cooking rather than after it's done
